summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd founded Bumble to create a safer, more respectful online dating environment by requiring women to make the first move. She believes that social networks must enforce rules to ensure accountability and combat misogyny, and she views Bumble not just as a dating app, but as a brand dedicated to empowering women and fostering mutual respect.

principles.statement: Social networks must enforce rules to ensure user accountability.
principles.rationale: Without rules, people lose their kindness behind screens, and real-life behavior dangerously starts to mimic toxic social media behavior.
principles.source_quote: I don’t know why the leaders of social networks have overlooked the idea of rules. Real-life behavior is becoming more reflective of social media instead of vice versa, and that’s a dangerous thing.

principles.statement: Never settle in relationships just to meet a timeline.
principles.rationale: Forcing a relationship just to be married by a certain age leads to dating people who are fundamentally wrong for you.
principles.source_quote: Do not date someone if it’s not right... And don’t settle. This settling thing is insane.

principles.statement: Feminism is about equality, compassion, and lifting each other up.
principles.rationale: Rather than being anti-male, feminism helps women and men build true partnerships and releases everyone from polarized gender roles.
principles.source_quote: I wanted to take a kinder path—to lift one another up, not tear one another down.

mental_models.name: Digital Etiquette / Online Manners
mental_models.description: Treating social network design as a system of etiquette where users are held accountable for their words and actions, rather than a lawless free-for-all.
mental_models.example: Implementing a 24-hour countdown for both women to message and men to respond, banning shirtless mirror selfies, and requiring photo verification to enforce good behavior.

signature_quotes.text: I don’t know why the leaders of social networks have overlooked the idea of rules.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the lack of accountability and the rise of toxic behavior on the internet.

signature_quotes.text: Do not date someone if it’s not right... And don’t settle. This settling thing is insane.
signature_quotes.context: Advising friends and users against forcing relationships just to meet societal age expectations.

signature_quotes.text: Misogyny has hit epidemic proportions. And I truly believe there is going to be an ongoing revolution that will not only tame the snake but cut off its head.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her motivation for getting out of bed every day and building Bumble.

signature_quotes.text: Bumble is not a dating app. Bumble is a brand that empowers women and empowers men to respect women.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the broader mission of her company beyond just matchmaking.

anti_patterns.description: Building social networks without behavioral rules.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It allows users to hide behind screens and lose their 'kindness gene,' leading to toxic behavior that eventually bleeds into real life.

anti_patterns.description: Giving users unlimited time to respond to a message.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It removes urgency and accountability. Bumble initially gave men unlimited time to respond to women's first messages, which Wolfe Herd admitted was a 'mistake' because it led to inaction.

anti_patterns.description: Settling for a partner to meet a societal timeline.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It forces people into relationships with individuals who are not right for them, simply because they want to be married by a certain age.
